Oracc: SAAo/SAA01 — The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West

SAA 01 006. (no title) (ABL 0298)[via saao/saa01]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[a-bat] ⸢LUGAL⸣ a-na ma-ši-[pa-a]

(1) [The ki]ng's word to Ašipâ: I am [we]ll, you can be glad.

o 22

[DI]-⸢mu⸣ ia-a-ši

(2) [The day] you see [thi]s letter, [...] Nabû-šeziban[ni ......] is surrounding [......

o 33

[ŠÀ-ba]-⸢ka⸣ lu DÙG.GA-ka

o 44

[UD-mu] ⸢ša⸣ e-gír-tú

o 55

[an-ni]-⸢tú⸣ ta-mar-u-ni

o 66

[x x x x]+⸢x⸣ mdPA—še-zib-an-[ni]

o 77

[x x x x] ⸢i?⸣-lab-bu*-ni*

o 88

[x x x x x x x]+⸢x⸣

rest broken away

(Rest destroyed)

Reverse
rr completely broken away

Adapted from Simo Parpola, The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West (State Archives of Assyria, 1), 1987. Lemmatised by Mikko Luukko, 2009-11, as part of the AHRC-funded research project “Mechanisms of Communication in an Ancient Empire: The Correspondence between the King of Assyria and his Magnates in the 8th Century BC” (AH/F016581/1; University College London) directed by Karen Radner. The annotated edition is released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/saao/P334190/.
